title: 096 phase 004 spec has mismatched anchors and fails strict validation
scopeProof: Anchor balance check across 42 sampled 096, 098/003, and system-spec-kit template files found this as the only mismatch; strict validate on 096/004 exits 2 with ANCHORS_VALID and SPEC_DOC_SUFFICIENCY errors.
claim: 096/004 is not maintainable as a valid spec document because machine-readable anchors are malformed.
counterevidenceSought: Checked related templates and specs for same-class anchor mismatch and ran the official strict validator for the affected spec folder.
alternativeExplanation: Could be harmless prose if anchors were not tooling inputs, but the official validator consumes them and fails.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if validator logic is intentionally changed to ignore nested/mismatched anchors and the spec folder validates strictly.
recommendation: Repair the 096/004 anchor block, then rerun validate.sh for the spec folder with --strict.

title: Deferred findings are omitted from _memory.continuity.blockers
scopeProof: All three deferred-item focus packets expose narrative followups but keep blockers empty in _memory.continuity.
claim: Deferred work is human-readable but not carried by the continuity surface that resume and memory workflows read first.
counterevidenceSought: Checked limitations, deviations, and followups in the same files for alternate surfacing. Prose exists; continuity blockers remain empty.
alternativeExplanation: blockers could mean only phase-local blockers, but P1-007 is still active in re-review and affects release readiness.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the continuity schema defines blockers as phase-local only and another indexed machine-readable field is shown to carry these follow-ons.
recommendation: Populate _memory.continuity.blockers or an equivalent machine-readable follow-on field for deferred items, especially P1-007 and unresolved P2-004.

title: 096 resource-map still contains sed-induced tautological rename headings
scopeProof: 098/003 repaired spec/description files but did not list resource-map.md; resource-map symlink rows still preserve old/new target distinction, so the headings can be repaired from local context.
recommendation: Restore singular-to-plural headings in resource-map.md for skills, agents, and commands, or annotate that the headings describe post-rename state rather than source-to-target migration.

name: template_anchor_health
notes: Manifest templates were included in a 42-file anchor balance check; no template mismatches were found.

name: rcaf_template_hygiene
notes: Current sk-doc template paths use assets/testing_playbook, not the stale prompt-pack path assets/templates; template and creation-reference guidance agree on natural-human default with RCAF as orchestrator exception.

name: cross_cli_doc_consistency
notes: All four CLI skills consistently reference the shared memory handback procedure. Prior P1-021 remains a validator-resolution issue, not authored target drift.
